headline: Why Shares of QuantumScape, First Solar, and ChargePoint Plunged in October
summary: After a bullish September when markets raced higher, investor sentiment headed south in October, resulting in the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) and Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI) tumbling 1% and 1.3%, respectively.  Several alternative energy stocks, however, suffered even steeper declines.  According to data provided by S&P Global Market Intelligence, First Solar (NASDAQ: FSLR), a leading manufacturer of solar panels, plunged 22%, while solid state battery specialist QuantumScape (NYSE: QS) and ChargePoint (NYSE: CHPT), a leader in electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure, dropped 10.4% and 12.4%, respectively.

headline: Why GE Vernova is this analysts energy sector pick under Trump
summary: President-elect Donald Trump has picked Liberty Energy (LBRT) CEO Chris Wright as his pick for secretary of the Department of Energy (DOE). Wright also sits on the board of nuclear energy provider Oklo Inc. (OKLO). William Blair group head of energy and sustainability research sector Jed Dorsheimer joins Julie Hyman and Madison Mills on Market Domination Overtime to discuss what stocks within the energy sector benefit the most from a second Trump administration. Wright is a fantastic pick [for DOE secretary] in terms of GE Vernova (GEV), Dorsheimer says, adding that the company is the worlds leader in energy in terms of its market share making natural gas turbines and switchgear substations. Watch the video above for Dorsheimers perspective on Trump-era energy policies, the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) data centers, how Trumps proposed Department of Government Efficiency Commission (DOGE), co-led by Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy could affect the future of nuclear energy, and more. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime here. This post was written by Naomi Buchanan.
